page: Microsoft Azure E4ns_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Env6 server family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, configured with 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores with hyper-threading. The system features 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on Premium Storage capabilities for remote disk attachments.

Benchmark data indicates a divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is average at 1916.18, and single-threaded gzip compression is strong at 31.08 GB/sec. However, multi-core stress-ng performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression are weak. Memory bandwidth is also in the lower tier, with uncached read speeds at 31.34 GB/sec and write speeds at 23.07 GB/sec. Database and Redis SET operations also benchmark in the lower quartile, while static web serving throughput is average at 7.48 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E4ns_v6 offers a high memory-to-vCPU density suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high parallel compute scaling. It is positioned as a network-optimized instance. Given the hardware profile and benchmark results, this server is best suited for memory-optimized workloads, moderate web serving, single-threaded processing tasks, and lightweight database or caching applications where memory capacity is prioritized over raw multi-core execution speed.
description: The Microsoft Azure E4ns_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Env6 family, running on a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz. It features 4 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB of memory per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on remote Premium Storage. Benchmarks show strong single-threaded performance, such as gzip compression, but weaker multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth. Database operations and Redis SET performance are also relatively low. This network-optimized server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand intense multi-threaded compute, making it suitable for memory-optimized applications, moderate web serving, and single-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4ns_v6 is a memory-optimized, network-optimized virtual machine featuring a 4-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor, 32 GB of RAM, and Premium Storage capability without local disks.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4ns_v6 is an Intel-powered, network-optimized VM with 4 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, designed for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and high memory-to-core density for network-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
Network-optimized architecture with Premium Storage support
Strong single-threaded performance but weaker multi-core scaling
No local storage or GPU resources included
Best suited for memory-intensive workloads and moderate web serving
